---
title: "Historical databases at Virginia Tech"
---

Use the table below to find databases with historical primary sources by geographic region and time period. Use the drop-down menus to choose a region or time period and the search box to search by database name.

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(reactable)
library(htmltools)
library(crosstalk)
library(jsonlite)
library(bslib)

urls_tbl <- read_csv("data/urls.csv")
geo_tidy <- read_csv("data/geography.csv")
period_tidy <- read_csv("data/period.csv")
```

```{r}
geo_nested <- geo_tidy |>
  group_by(database) |>
  summarise(geography = list(geography))

period_nested <- period_tidy |>
  group_by(database) |>
  summarise(period = list(period))

data_tbl <- urls_tbl |>
  left_join(geo_nested, by = "database") |>
  left_join(period_nested, by = "database")

# Shared data for the table
shared_data <- SharedData$new(data_tbl)

# Shared data for the Crosstalk filters. This is a separate shared data object
# so we can filter on columns that aren't in the table. The same group is used
# to link the two datasets together.
shared_databases <- SharedData$new(data_tbl, group = shared_data$groupName())

tbl <- reactable(
  shared_data,
  columns = list(
    # Or using raw HTML
    link = colDef(
      name = "Database",
      html = TRUE
    ),
    database = colDef(show = FALSE),
    url = colDef(show = FALSE),
    geography = colDef(show = FALSE),
    period = colDef(show = FALSE)
  ),
  searchable = TRUE
)
```


```{r}
# Custom Crosstalk select filter. This is a single-select input that works
# on columns containing multiple values per row (list columns).
select_filter <- function(id, label, shared_data, group, choices = NULL,
                          width = "100%", class = "filter-input") {
  values <- shared_data$data()[[group]]
  keys <- shared_data$key()
  if (is.list(values)) {
    # Multiple values per row
    flat_keys <- unlist(mapply(rep, keys, sapply(values, length)))
    keys_by_value <- split(flat_keys, unlist(values), drop = TRUE)
    choices <- if (is.null(choices)) sort(unique(unlist(values))) else choices
  } else {
    # Single value per row
    keys_by_value <- split(seq_along(keys), values, drop = TRUE)
    choices <- if (is.null(choices)) sort(unique(values)) else choices
  }

  script <- sprintf("
    window['__ct__%s'] = (function() {
      const handle = new window.crosstalk.FilterHandle('%s')
      const keys = %s
      return {
        filter: function(value) {
          if (!value) {
            handle.clear()
          } else {
            handle.set(keys[value])
          }
        }
      }
    })()
  ", id, shared_data$groupName(), toJSON(keys_by_value))

  div(
    class = class,
    tags$label(`for` = id, label),
    tags$select(
      id = id,
      onchange = sprintf("window['__ct__%s'].filter(this.value)", id),
      style = sprintf("width: %s", validateCssUnit(width)),
      tags$option(value = "", "All"),
      lapply(choices, function(value) tags$option(value = value, value))
    ),
    tags$script(HTML(script))
  )
}
```


```{r}
page_fluid(
  layout_columns(
    select_filter("filter_geo", "Geographic region", shared_databases, "geography"),
    select_filter("filter_period", "Period", shared_databases, "period",
                  choices = c("Medieval", "Early Modern", "1701 - 1800",
                              "1801 - 1900", "1901 - 2000", "2001 - Present"))
  ),
  tbl
)
```

Resources for this page are available on [GitHub](https://github.com/jessesadler/historical-databases).
